sessions.intent: Move template stamping from a post-parse walk onto the read: the reader hands the type its template mode at construction, the type decides (it owns its holes). Trust rides the reader, not the content.

before.assumptions: ReadContext is the natural mode carrier (IReader work landed). text ctor's canTemplate:true hardcoded "plang" mode-blind — the bug. Goal is the only inherently-authored read.
before.risk: Security-critical: the mode must NOT ride the shared _inbound Wire (also reads runtime messages). FromWire rebuilds from already-parsed values — its seam can't delete blind. Two goal-load paths (goal.list via plang serializer, GoalCall via catalog options).

actions.context: ReadContext gains Template (string?). text ctor: bool canTemplate -> string? template; Template = (template != null && HasHoles) ? template : null. The type owns the holes-decision so HasVariableReference stays correct.
text.Read + text/Default.Read pass ctx.Template (mode-gated). path keeps "plang" (no read-mode at its ctor; mode-gating path deferred, behavior preserved).

Wire gains a Template ctor param, feeds it into the ReadContext for ITypeReader.Read. Plang serializer gains a dedicated _authored options (template="plang"); Deserialize<goal> routes to it (goal is the inherently-authored type). The single trusted construction site.
Security test: same %ref% bytes stamp under authored mode, stay literal under runtime mode, holeless never stamps. Updated 5 DataTests canTemplate:true -> "plang".

after.status: Increment 1 landed — born-with-template on the read path, mode-gated, proven. Seams still in place (idempotent); StampedForm removal + path-2 read-stamp + container slots + FromWire are later increments.
after.health: green — dev.sh full all 6 projects green (counts identical to baseline; one flaky Types failure cleared on re-run). Security test passes. Runtime template-rendering suite (789) green.
after.notes: The mode rides _authored only; _inbound (runtime messages) stays mode-off. GoalCall path 2 (catalog options) still seam-stamps until its read is routed through _authored too.

sessions.intent: Migrate hand-built goal/step executors in PLang.Tests/Types to load goals through the real read path (Make + RealGoalLoad.ViaChannel + TestApp.Create), matching the proven pilot pattern.

before.assumptions: Named TypeKindStrict/PathTests files would contain goal/step executors built via new PrAction/new Goal and run via RunGoalAsync/steps.RunAsync — the migration anti-pattern.
before.risk: Strict/kind type-mismatch tests intentionally hand-build to hit a precise validation layer; migrating could move where the failure surfaces.

actions.context: Examined all seven. None are goal/step executors: each uses TestAction.Create(...).RunAsync (the sanctioned single-action isolation helper, which already applies StampTemplates) or direct type/FilePath/ValidateBuild calls. The migration target is nested new PrAction/new Goal run via RunGoalAsync/steps.RunAsync (the pilot's before-state) — absent here. The set tests also pass explicit type/kind/strict params that Make.Param cannot express and that probe precise mint/validation layers — squarely the leave-alone caveat. No code changed.

after.status: No migrations performed — examination concluded none of the named files are in-scope goal/step executors.
after.health: green — PLang.Tests/Types builds with 0 CS errors and tests pass (failed: 0).
after.notes: TestAction.Create + action.RunAsync is action-isolation, not the hand-built-Goal+RunGoalAsync anti-pattern the migration targets. Kind/strict are inexpressible via Make.Param, reinforcing leave-alone.

sessions.intent: Migrate PLang.Tests/Data executor tests to RealGoalLoad.ViaChannel + Make + TestApp.Create

before.assumptions: Make.Goal hardcodes Path=root; no Defaults seam; steps.RunAsync machinery tests stay hand-built per PlangRuntimeTests precedent
before.risk: custom capture handlers + born-typing through read path may diverge

actions.context: Migrated 3 RunGoalAsync behavioral tests (ExecutesSteps, WithActor, ByName) to RealGoalLoad.ViaChannel + Make + TestApp.Create. Left steps.RunAsync machinery, cancelled, malformed variable.get, constructor/dispose/actor tests hand-built. Kept MakeStep helpers.
Migrated programmatic + 5 ResolveValue_* tests. Left 3 Defaults_* tests (Make has no Defaults seam). Kept MakeStep/MakeStepWithDefaults helpers + custom CapturingWriteHandler.

Left unchanged. PrPipeline: fixtures load real .pr off disk; hand-built ones need subfolder goal Path which Make.Goal hardcodes to root. Goal/Step/Variables: structural/Variables-collection unit tests, no goal execution.

after.notes: Data project build clean (only pre-existing CS8981 lowercase-type warnings). Suite: failed:1 = pre-existing Diff_DiffModeOverLargeListDoesNotOom (perf/OOM, unrelated). StartGoalTests 9/9 + EngineTests 35/35 pass. No commit per task instruction. Read-path gap: Make.Goal hardcodes Path=/{name}.goal so subfolder-path-resolution tests cannot migrate.
